subject: Gaming
title: The 1993 Congressional Hearings
description: A short lesson to help you apply The 1993 Congressional Hearings.

hook.text: Mortal Kombat's spine-ripping fatalities triggered US Senate hearings. Congress wanted to ban violent games.

buildup.text: Senators Joseph Lieberman and Herb Kohl led hearings after parents saw Mortal Kombat's graphic violence.

discovery.text: The industry was given an ultimatum: create a rating system or face government regulation.

twist.text: Instead of a ban, the ESRB was born. The industry self-regulated, avoiding government censorship entirely.

climax.text: The ESRB became the model for game ratings worldwide. A crisis created a system that still works today.

punchline.text: Congress threatened a ban. The industry built a rating system instead.

quiz.question: What did the 1993 Congressional hearings lead to?
options: The creation of the ESRB rating system
A complete ban on violent games
